channel.title: Film-Analysis on Rathtakrit
channel.description: Recent content in Film-Analysis on Rathtakrit

item.title: American Psycho
item.description: Notes and first-pass analysis of American Psycho as satire, performance, and social critique.
item.content:encoded:
Why This Film Stays With Me

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. This draft captures first impressions before I turn it into a full essay.

  • The film feels stylish and absurd at the same time.
  • The narration is funny, but also deeply unsettling.
  • Every “perfect” routine reveals insecurity and emptiness.

Quick Thesis (Working Draft)

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

  • American Psycho is less about “who did what” and more about performance.
  • Identity is treated like a brand package: clothes, cards, restaurants, status.
  • Violence is framed as part of a culture already dehumanized by wealth and competition.

Scenes I Want To Break Down

  • Business card scene: ego, status anxiety, and masculinity.
  • Morning routine monologue: body, image, and self-construction.
  • Restaurant sequences: hierarchy, trend worship, and social theater.
  • Final confession: ambiguity, denial, and institutional emptiness.

Questions To Explore

  • Is Bateman uniquely monstrous, or just the most honest product of his world?
  • How does the film use humor to make criticism sharper?
  • Does ambiguity in the ending weaken or strengthen the message?

References To Add Later

  • Director interviews and production context.
  • Bret Easton Ellis novel comparisons.
  • Critical essays on capitalism, masculinity, and satire.
